Title: Mutations in MIR396e and MIR396f increase grain size and modulate shoot architecture in rice

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 mutagenesis; northern blot; RT-qPCR; field phenotyping; RNA-seq; metabolomics; phytohormone quantification
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: mevalonic acid; gibberellins (GA3, GA4, GA7; GA24 precursor)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Gibberellins promote organ elongation; terpenoid products can contribute to pollination, defense, abiotic stress tolerance, and medicinal value as discussed
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of MIR396e together with MIR396f increases grain size and 1000-grain weight, enlarges panicles, elevates mevalonic acid and bioactive GA levels in leaves, promotes leaf elongation, and contributes to shortened upper internodes
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Natural paddy field conditions in Hangzhou and Hainan; humid weather in Hangzhou aggravated panicle exsertion-related fertility defects
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising gene-editing target to increase grain size and yield, but deployment requires correction of shortened internodes/panicle exsertion defect, potentially by combining with eui1 or hox12 as discussed
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Shortened upper internodes, panicle exsertion defect, reduced fertility/seed filling under humid conditions; higher-order mutants can show severe fertility reduction
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GRF3; GRF4/GS2/GL2 and other miR396-targeted GRFs (inferred targets)
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CRISPR/Cas9 sgRNA expression cassettes; specific promoters not reported
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; metabolomics; phytohormone LC-MS/MS; accession numbers for omics datasets not provided
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MIR396e alone showed little visible phenotype; major effects were revealed in mir396ef double mutants, indicating redundancy with MIR396f
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MIR396e is one of the two principal MIR396 family members whose simultaneous disruption with MIR396f caused enlarged grains and panicles. 2. mir396ef leaves accumulated dramatically more mevalonic acid and higher GA3/GA4/GA7, consistent with enhanced GA-mediated leaf elongation. 3. The mutation also contributed to shortened upper internodes and panicle exsertion defects, limiting breeding utility unless architecture is corrected.

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 mutagenesis; northern blot; RT-qPCR; field phenotyping; RNA-seq; metabolomics; phytohormone quantification
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: mevalonic acid; gibberellins (GA3, GA4, GA7; GA24 precursor)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Gibberellins promote organ elongation; terpenoid products can contribute to pollination, defense, abiotic stress tolerance, and medicinal value as discussed
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of MIR396f together with MIR396e increases grain size and yield potential, elevates mevalonic acid and GA accumulation in leaves, promotes leaf elongation, and contributes to altered shoot architecture with shortened upper internodes
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Natural paddy field conditions in Hangzhou and Hainan; humid weather in Hangzhou aggravated panicle exsertion-related fertility defects
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful gene-editing target for rice improvement, but practical use requires overcoming internode shortening/panicle exsertion defects
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Shortened upper internodes, panicle enclosure, lower seed filling/fertility in humid environments; redundancy with other MIR396 members for fertility
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GRF3; GRF4/GS2/GL2 and other miR396-targeted GRFs (inferred targets)
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CRISPR/Cas9 sgRNA expression cassettes; specific promoters not reported
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; metabolomics; phytohormone LC-MS/MS; accession numbers for omics datasets not provided
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MIR396f alone showed little visible phenotype; strong effects emerged in the mir396ef double mutant, supporting functional redundancy
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MIR396f acts redundantly with MIR396e as a key regulator of grain size and shoot architecture in rice. 2. Double mutation strongly increased mevalonic acid and bioactive GAs in leaves and lengthened leaves. 3. The same mutation shortened upper internodes and caused environment-sensitive yield penalties despite yield gains in Hainan.

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq and RT-qPCR correlation analysis in developing uppermost internodes
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in developing uppermost internode; reduced in mir396ef
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing uppermost internode; reduced in mir396ef
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced CYP96B4 expression is correlated with suppressed internode elongation in mir396ef, apparently independent of GA pathway changes
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential architecture-correction target when combining mir396ef with modifications that restore internode elongation
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced expression is associated with shortened internodes; prior reports cited link loss of CYP96B4 to carbohydrate and lipid metabolism defects
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This gene is not the primary edited target but is a core experimentally implicated downstream factor explaining internode phenotype
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP96B4 expression was markedly decreased in mir396ef developing uppermost internodes. 2. The internode defect could not be explained by obvious GA-pathway defects, implicating CYP96B4-associated mechanisms instead. 3. The paper links reduced CYP96B4 with downregulation of carbohydrate and lipid metabolism genes and suppressed internode elongation.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq expression profiling; RT-qPCR/corroborative expression analysis of miR396 targets
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest expressed among miR396-targeted GRFs in leaves and developing uppermost internodes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaves and developing uppermost internodes; upregulated in mir396ef
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: mevalonic acid; gibberellin-associated leaf growth output
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly implicated downstream target contributing to altered leaf and internode elongation in mir396ef
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Post-transcriptional repression by miR396; derepressed in mir396ef
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Indirect control of growth traits linked to GA-associated elongation and grain development
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Suggested major downstream effector among miR396-targeted GRFs because it is most highly expressed and strongly upregulated in mir396ef
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential downstream regulatory target for tuning grain size and architecture, though direct engineering value is not established in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Its exact mechanism in regulating leaf and internode elongation remains unresolved
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GRF3 was among several miR396-targeted GRFs upregulated in mir396ef. 2. It showed the highest expression in both leaves and developing uppermost internodes and stronger induction than most other GRFs. 3. The paper therefore suggests GRF3 is intensely involved in the altered elongation phenotypes caused by mir396ef.
